message: You've been coming around often lately.
there are no signs of anyone, just me and the bartender.
The lights are off, and no music is playing. It’s a terribly lonely sight with just the bartender silently polishing glasses.
It’s a bar that can't be called beautiful, to say the least.
and he isn’t dressed flashy like most of the customers.
I wonder if he has been working hard from this time until closing? He doesn't look like the kind of tough guy who could do that.
He doesn't seem particularly interested in me; it just feels like he’s looking over here out of obligation and making small talk.
is on break. If you want to see him, just go to his house.
So, how have you been lately?
I'm totally out of it. I’ve become useless.
For someone in business, it’s a real pain, that guy.
Well, he’s been really down lately. It’s no good looking that gloomy. You should talk to him.
He's going through a tough time too.
I get that, but just because a woman left him,
from my perspective, he’s being a softy.
He’s a good guy, but he doesn’t open up much. That’s why I can’t help him.
Sure, there’s that, but I feel like he talks to me relatively more.
Of course, that’s because you guys are friends.
Do you think he can get back to work?
I haven’t seen Ariana around. She used to drop by to tease him.
It’s true, she probably isn't coming.
I guess there's no way she would come.
What's going on? Isn’t it just some fight?
I don’t really get it, but I think it’s just that
he's too spoiled and got carried away. That's not really anyone else's business.
Ever since Ariana stopped coming, the customers have stayed away, and it's a bit
It seems he was incredibly shocked by her death.
Has he finally reached his limit?
Should I go see him in his room?
This man doesn't know that Ariana =
Looking back now, during the times we were here,
it might've been when she was the liveliest.
